strengths: Effective use of communication skills: surveys and interviews directly shaped design choices like real-time feedback and color schemes
Research skills guided component selection and coding strategy, supported by expert insights
Well-organized revision notes demonstrate strong self-management
Research organizer links inquiry questions to credible sources for focused investigation
Application of C-C-C and OPVL methods shows critical evaluation of information
Reflection on how research skills overcame challenges indicates growing metacognitive awareness
weaknesses: Mind-map lacks explicit iterative reflection or self-management checkpoints
Raw interview transcript is verboseâkey recommendations should be summarized for clarity
Survey results table needs context (sample size, question wording) and clear axis labels for transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The work shows robust application of ATL research and communication skills with strong evidence of impact on design and learning. Refining the presentation of research artifactsâsummarizing interviews, clarifying survey methodology, and mapping process checkpointsâwill enhance clarity and demonstrate comprehensive skill integration.

strengths: Explicit learning goal with measurable criteria for motion-detection accuracy and user validation
Use of primary interviews to ground component choices and demonstrate feasibility
Clear, convincing personal interest linked to family health experiences
Comprehensive description of the intended product detailing components, sensor integration, and user engagement
Detailed, sequenced project timeline tying tasks to ATL skills and success criteria
weaknesses: Material criteria lack quantitative benchmarks (e.g., flexibility tolerance, durability cycles)
Success-criteria table omits measurable targets for functionality such as sensor accuracy thresholds and engagement metrics
Environmental impact criterion has no specific testing procedures or emissions targets
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section excels at defining a clear, measurable learning goal, grounding decisions in expert feedback, and outlining a comprehensive product and timeline. To strengthen feasibility and evaluation, it needs more precise, quantifiable criteria for materials, functionality, and environmental impact.

strengths: Final evaluation balances achievements and improvement areas, showing honest self-assessment
Clear reflection on how research and communication skills contributed to learning outcomes
Candid discussion of challenges (sensor calibration, budgeting) and strategies used to overcome them
Photographic documentation provides powerful visual evidence supporting reflections
weaknesses: Evaluation table addresses only one success criterionâneeds expansion to cover all defined criteria with supporting evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection is thorough in linking ATL skill development to project growth and acknowledging obstacles overcome. To complete the reflective cycle, the product should be evaluated systematically against every success criterion, with specific examples for each.

annotations.comment: Your final evaluation balances strengths and areas for improvement, demonstrating honest self-assessment and commitment to iterative refinement.
The evaluation table begins to address only one success criterion. Expand this section to evaluate the product against all defined criteria with supporting evidence for each (C1/C2).
You reflect on how research and communication skills contributed to your learning, showing clear links between ATL development and project outcomes.

Your learning goal is explicitâdeveloping proficiency in C++ and Arduino with measurable criteria for motion detection accuracy and user validation. This clarity sets a solid foundation for planning.
Incorporating primary interviews to inform sensor and microcontroller choices demonstrates feasibility and shows you are grounding your plan in expert feedback.
Material criteria describe components but lack quantitative tests (e.g., glove flexibility tolerance, durability over cycles). Define measurable benchmarks to ensure consistency and quality.
The mindâmap outlines your research structure but could better illustrate iterative reflection or self-management checkpoints to fully demonstrate skill integration.
The communicationâskills section shows clear use of surveys and interviews to align product design with user needs, giving evidence of ATL skills applied to your product.
The raw interview transcript is lengthy and repetitive. Summarize key recommendations in a concise format to enhance clarity and focus on actionable design insights.
You establish a convincing personal interest by linking your relativeâs experiences and hospital visits to your project, strengthening the motivation behind your learning goal.
You articulate how research skills guided component selection and coding strategy, supported by expert interviews and structured inquiry questions, showing effective ATL application.
Your revision notes are comprehensive and wellâorganized, illustrating self-management by providing a personal resource to reinforce key technical and physiotherapy concepts.
Your intended product description is comprehensive, detailing glove components, sensor integration, and user engagement methodsâthis precision supports a focused development plan.

The success criteria table addresses aesthetics but omits clear, measurable criteria for functionality (e.g., sensor accuracy thresholds) and user engagement tests. Expand this section to cover all technical requirements.

The environmental criterion lacks a defined testing procedure or specific emissions target. Introduce measurable checks (e.g., energy consumption per hour) to fully validate your minimal impact claim.
Your detailed project timeline sequences tasks, ATL skill use, and success criteria clearly, demonstrating strong planning, resource management, and feasibility awareness.
Your research organizer table succinctly links primary and secondary questions to credible sources, enhancing focus and efficiency in your investigation.
Applying C-C-C and OPVL evaluation methods to your website and video sources demonstrates critical thinking and ensures you use reliable, balanced information.
You effectively applied insights from expert interviews to refine design choices such as color scheme and sensor selection, showcasing real impact of ATL research skills.
You leverage survey data to make targeted design choicesârealâtime feedback and color preferencesâdemonstrating impact of ATL communication skills on product quality.
Your reflection on how research skills overcame challenges and refined focus illustrates growing metacognitive awareness and strengthens your Applying Skills strand.
Your survey results table is informative but lacks clear axis labels and methodological notes. Adding context (sample size, question wording) will improve transparency and reproducibility.
Your reflection on using communication skills shows depth by linking survey and expert feedback to design, illustrating how ATL skills shaped both process and product.
You candidly describe challenges like sensor calibration and budgeting, and explain strategies you used to overcome themâthis level of reflection demonstrates solid personal growth.
Photographic documentation of development stages and the final product adds powerful visual evidence of your process and supports your reflective analysis.
